summary: A mostly solid soundboard capture of a fiery 1976 show, highlighted by an inspired second set with soaring "Eyes", hot Help‑Slip, and the best "High Time" ever heard despite a few patchy audience inserts.
review: The performance shines especially in the second set – "Eyes" prefigures Englishtown, the Help>Slip jam is on fire, and Jerry delivers a pure vocal on an outstanding "High Time." The recording is primarily soundboard quality with clear guitar tone and a notable quadraphonic bass effect, though it suffers from brief audience patches on "The Other One" and a one‑minute audio glitch at the end of "Eyes," as well as subdued vocals.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and highlight several standout songs; recording quality is generally good with only isolated issues, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard mix with sweet guitar tone and a quadraphonic bass effect. Minor flaws include a 1‑minute audio patch at the end of "Eyes," audience patches on "The Other One" (and possibly "Dew"), and vocals that suffer slightly.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid but unremarkable; the second set is highly inspired, with seamless transitions and powerful jams that make this show a 1976 gem.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s neck‑pickup on his Travis Bean gives a sweet tone; vocals are pure, especially on "High Time".
band_member_comments.Phil: A fantastic quadraphonic effect on the bass is noted by listeners.
